citations.description: test_e2e_offline_produces_verdict: stub-runner integration test that never calls sidecar.emit() or checks emitted event
test_reader_accepts_both_v1_and_v2_schemas: real emit path used, but with hand-constructed findings, not driven by a stub-LLM review
test_build_payload_v2_persists_evidence_scenarios_and_thresholds: unit test of build_payload only, no runner, no real emit path

findings.criterion: Running `review-plan` emits a `REVIEW_RESULT` event whose `schema` is `plan_review_result_v2` and whose every persisted finding carries `evidence`, `scenarios`, `block_threshold`, and `blocking_enabled`. Oracle: an in-process integration test drives a review with a stub LLM runner and asserts on the emitted event JSON via the real sidecar emit path (test-design §3 producer/consumer shape).
findings.detail: No integration test found that satisfies the oracle: drives a full plan review with a stub LLM runner AND asserts on the emitted sidecar event JSON (via the real git-backed emit path) to confirm every finding carries `evidence`, `scenarios`, `block_threshold`, and `blocking_enabled`. The closest tests split the concern: `test_e2e_offline_produces_verdict` (test_plan_review_workflow.py) runs a full review with a stub FakeRunner but never calls `sidecar.emit()` or inspects the emitted event JSON. `test_reader_accepts_both_v1_and_v2_schemas` (test_sidecar_reader.py:163) uses the real emit path and checks v2 fields, but with manually constructed findings — not the output of a stub-LLM-driven review. `test_audit_plan_review_v2.py` unit-tests `build_payload` directly (no runner, no emit). The stated oracle (§3 producer/consumer shape: a real review run → real emit → assert on emitted JSON) is not implemented.
findings.remediation: Add an integration test (e.g. in tests/interfaces/store/) that: (1) creates a rebar_repo ticket, (2) calls `produce_plan_review_verdict` (or `run_workflow`) with a FakeRunner whose findings carry `evidence`/`scenarios` and whose decisions carry `block_threshold`/`blocking_enabled`, (3) calls `sidecar.emit(verdict, repo_root=str(rebar_repo))`, then (4) reads back the emitted REVIEW_RESULT event JSON from the ticket dir and asserts `schema == 'plan_review_result_v2'` and every finding carries the four new fields.

data.remediation: How to resolve the unmet criteria: for each one, decide whether the work is genuinely incomplete or simply undocumented. If a requirement is already satisfied but the proof is not discoverable from the code alone, add a comment to this ticket that documents the evidence — cite the concrete artifacts that meet the requirement (file paths and line ranges, commands and their output, links, or the reasoning that ties the work to the criterion). The completion verifier reads this ticket's comments, so evidence you record there is taken into account on the next verification. For any criterion whose work is genuinely unfinished, complete it, then re-verify.
